appraisal_events.evidence: « ...presque sans dissimulation... il plongea un regard attentif, sérieux, absorbé, presque soucieux, dans les profondeurs du corsage, et ses narines... palpitèrent comme un papillon... »; « Mme de Surgis elle-même, quoique gênée, étouffa une respiration profonde. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator insists on Swann's indiscretion of desire in public, showing a loss of control and decorum that makes the marquise uncomfortable. This staging locally diminishes the esteem attached to Swann.

status_effects.explanation: The ostentation of his desire, described as barely concealed and socially embarrassing, makes him appear less deserving and controlled in the eyes of the reader and those present.
